description: A synchronous FIFO (First-In-First-Out) buffer with configurable data width and depth. Provides full, empty, almost_full, and almost_empty status flags along with an accurate count of entries. Supports simultaneous read/write operations with proper priority handling.

modules.name: sync_fifo
modules.description: Top-level synchronous FIFO module implementing circular buffer with read/write pointers, status flags, and entry counting

ports.name: clk
ports.description: System clock - all operations synchronous to rising edge

ports.name: rst_n
ports.description: Active-low asynchronous reset - clears FIFO and resets all pointers

ports.name: wr_en
ports.description: Write enable - when high, writes wr_data to FIFO if not full

ports.name: rd_en
ports.description: Read enable - when high, reads data from FIFO if not empty

ports.name: wr_data
ports.description: Write data input - data to be written into FIFO

ports.name: rd_data
ports.description: Read data output - data read from FIFO head

ports.name: full
ports.description: FIFO full flag - asserted when count equals DEPTH

ports.name: empty
ports.description: FIFO empty flag - asserted when count equals 0

ports.name: almost_full
ports.description: Almost full flag - asserted when count equals DEPTH-1

ports.name: almost_empty
ports.description: Almost empty flag - asserted when count equals 1

ports.name: count
ports.description: Current number of entries in FIFO (0 to DEPTH)

parameters.name: DATA_WIDTH
parameters.description: Width of data bus in bits - determines size of each FIFO entry

parameters.name: DEPTH
parameters.description: FIFO depth - maximum number of entries that can be stored

fsms.name: fifo_state_machine
fsms.description: Main FIFO state machine tracking fill level and controlling flag outputs based on current occupancy

fsms.name: pointer_management
fsms.description: Pointer management FSM controlling read and write pointer increments based on valid operations

key_signals.name: wr_ptr
key_signals.description: Write pointer - points to next write location in memory array

key_signals.name: rd_ptr
key_signals.description: Read pointer - points to next read location in memory array

key_signals.name: mem
key_signals.description: Internal memory array storing FIFO data

key_signals.name: count_reg
key_signals.description: Internal register tracking current number of entries

key_signals.name: wr_ptr_next
key_signals.description: Next write pointer value (with wraparound logic)

key_signals.name: rd_ptr_next
key_signals.description: Next read pointer value (with wraparound logic)

key_signals.name: count_next
key_signals.description: Next count value based on read/write operations

key_signals.name: wr_valid
key_signals.description: Internal signal indicating valid write operation (wr_en && !full) || (wr_en && rd_en && full)

key_signals.name: rd_valid
key_signals.description: Internal signal indicating valid read operation (rd_en && !empty) || (wr_en && rd_en && empty)

key_signals.name: wr_ptr_msb
key_signals.description: MSB of write pointer for wraparound detection

key_signals.name: rd_ptr_msb
key_signals.description: MSB of read pointer for wraparound detection

key_signals.name: ptr_equal
key_signals.description: Flag indicating read and write pointers are equal

key_signals.name: ptr_diff
key_signals.description: Difference between write and read pointers for count calculation

interfaces.name: fifo_write_interface
interfaces.description: Write-side interface for pushing data into FIFO

interfaces.name: fifo_read_interface
interfaces.description: Read-side interface for popping data from FIFO

interfaces.name: fifo_status_interface
interfaces.description: Status monitoring interface providing FIFO occupancy information

requirements.description: FIFO Ordering: Data shall be read in the same order it was written (first-in-first-out). Verify by writing known sequence and reading back in order.
Full Flag: The full flag shall be asserted when FIFO contains DEPTH entries and shall prevent further writes (unless simultaneous read).
Empty Flag: The empty flag shall be asserted when FIFO contains 0 entries and shall prevent further reads (unless simultaneous write).
Write When Full: Write operations shall be ignored when full is asserted unless there is a simultaneous read operation.
Read When Empty: Read operations shall be ignored when empty is asserted unless there is a simultaneous write operation.
Count Accuracy: The count signal shall accurately reflect the number of entries in the FIFO at all times (0 to DEPTH).
Almost Full: The almost_full flag shall be asserted when count equals (DEPTH-1) and deasserted otherwise.
Almost Empty: The almost_empty flag shall be asserted when count equals 1 and deasserted otherwise.
Simultaneous Operations: When wr_en and rd_en are both asserted: (a) If FIFO is empty, perform write only; (b) If FIFO is full, perform read only; (c) Otherwise, perform both operations.
Reset Behavior: Upon rst_n assertion, FIFO shall be cleared, empty=1, full=0, count=0, and all pointers reset to 0.
Synchronous Outputs: All outputs shall be synchronous to the rising edge of clk with no combinational paths from inputs to outputs.
Data Integrity: Data shall not be corrupted during storage or retrieval. Each write must be readable exactly once with correct value.
Pointer Wraparound: Read and write pointers shall correctly wrap around from (DEPTH-1) to 0 without data loss or corruption.
Flag Mutual Exclusion: Full and empty flags shall never be asserted simultaneously except during reset.
Count Bounds: Count shall never exceed DEPTH or go below 0. Count transitions shall be +1, -1, or 0 per cycle.
Back-to-Back Operations: FIFO shall support back-to-back write and read operations without requiring idle cycles.
Flag Timing: Status flags (full, empty, almost_full, almost_empty) shall update on the same clock cycle as the count changes.
Read Data Stability: rd_data shall remain stable when empty is asserted and no write occurs, or shall update to next valid entry on read.
Parameterization: FIFO shall function correctly for any DATA_WIDTH >= 1 and DEPTH >= 2.
Power-On State: After power-on and reset release, FIFO shall be in empty state with count=0.

corner_cases.description: Write to Full FIFO: Verify writes are ignored when full flag is asserted, no data corruption occurs, and count remains at DEPTH.
corner_cases.how_to_test: Fill FIFO to capacity, attempt additional writes while monitoring full flag, verify count stays at DEPTH and no pointers advance. Read back all data to verify integrity.

corner_cases.description: Read from Empty FIFO: Verify reads are ignored when empty flag is asserted, rd_data remains stable or shows last valid data, and count remains at 0.
corner_cases.how_to_test: Empty FIFO completely, attempt additional reads while monitoring empty flag, verify count stays at 0, rd_data doesn't change, and no pointers advance.

corner_cases.description: Simultaneous R/W When Full: Verify that simultaneous read/write when full performs only read operation, count decrements to DEPTH-1, and full flag deasserts.
corner_cases.how_to_test: Fill FIFO to capacity, assert both wr_en and rd_en simultaneously, verify only read occurs (count=DEPTH-1), write data is ignored, and full flag clears.

corner_cases.description: Simultaneous R/W When Empty: Verify that simultaneous read/write when empty performs only write operation, count increments to 1, and empty flag deasserts.
corner_cases.how_to_test: Empty FIFO completely, assert both wr_en and rd_en simultaneously, verify only write occurs (count=1), no read data is consumed, and empty flag clears.

corner_cases.description: Pointer Wraparound: Verify correct behavior when read/write pointers wrap around from (DEPTH-1) to 0, including data integrity and flag accuracy.
corner_cases.how_to_test: Write DEPTH entries, read DEPTH entries (forcing wraparound), write DEPTH more entries, read all back. Verify data integrity across wraparound boundary and flag correctness.

corner_cases.description: Rapid Fill/Empty Cycles: Verify stability during rapid transitions between full and empty states without intermediate stable states.
corner_cases.how_to_test: Alternate between filling FIFO completely and emptying it completely in rapid succession. Monitor for flag glitches, count errors, or data corruption.

corner_cases.description: Single Entry Operation: Verify correct behavior when FIFO has exactly 1 entry (almost_empty asserted, count=1).
corner_cases.how_to_test: Write one entry, verify almost_empty=1, empty=0, count=1. Read entry, verify transitions to empty. Test simultaneous R/W with count=1.

corner_cases.description: Reset During Operations: Verify clean recovery when reset asserted during active read/write operations at various fill levels.
corner_cases.how_to_test: Partially fill FIFO, assert reset during write operation. Verify all pointers reset, flags correct (empty=1, full=0), count=0. Repeat during read and simultaneous R/W.

corner_cases.description: Back-to-Back Operations: Verify correct behavior with consecutive write or read operations with no idle cycles between them.
corner_cases.how_to_test: Perform DEPTH consecutive writes without gaps, verify all data stored correctly. Perform DEPTH consecutive reads, verify all data retrieved in order. Test mixed back-to-back R/W.

corner_cases.description: Data Patterns: Verify integrity with various data patterns including all 0s, all 1s, random data, walking 1s, walking 0s, and alternating patterns.
corner_cases.how_to_test: Write each pattern type to FIFO, read back and compare. Test patterns at various fill levels. Verify no bit corruption or pattern-dependent failures.

corner_cases.description: Almost Full Boundary: Verify almost_full flag behavior at count=DEPTH-2 to DEPTH-1 transition and back.
corner_cases.how_to_test: Fill FIFO to DEPTH-2 entries, verify almost_full=0. Write one more (count=DEPTH-1), verify almost_full=1. Write/read to test transitions around this boundary.

corner_cases.description: Almost Empty Boundary: Verify almost_empty flag behavior at count=1 to 2 transition and back.
corner_cases.how_to_test: Write 2 entries, verify almost_empty=0. Read one (count=1), verify almost_empty=1. Write/read to test transitions around this boundary.

corner_cases.description: Minimum Depth FIFO: Verify correct operation with DEPTH=2 (minimum practical depth) where almost_full and full are adjacent states.
corner_cases.how_to_test: Instantiate FIFO with DEPTH=2, verify all operations, flags, and corner cases work correctly with minimal depth.

corner_cases.description: Maximum Data Width: Verify correct operation with large DATA_WIDTH values (e.g., 128, 256 bits) to test memory array implementation.
corner_cases.how_to_test: Instantiate FIFO with DATA_WIDTH=128 or 256, perform full test suite, verify no width-related issues in storage or retrieval.

corner_cases.description: Pointer Collision: Verify correct behavior when read and write pointers are equal in both empty and full conditions.
corner_cases.how_to_test: Monitor internal pointers. When empty (pointers equal), verify empty=1. Fill to full (pointers equal again), verify full=1. Ensure correct differentiation.

corner_cases.description: Count Increment/Decrement: Verify count correctly increments on write-only, decrements on read-only, and stays same on simultaneous R/W.
corner_cases.how_to_test: Test each scenario at various fill levels. Verify count changes by exactly +1, -1, or 0. Check for off-by-one errors.

corner_cases.description: Flag Coherency: Verify all flags (full, empty, almost_full, almost_empty) are mutually consistent with count value at all times.
corner_cases.how_to_test: Create checker that validates flag states against count: empty iff count=0, full iff count=DEPTH, almost_empty iff count=1, almost_full iff count=DEPTH-1.

corner_cases.description: Asynchronous Reset Timing: Verify reset can be asserted/deasserted at any point in clock cycle and FIFO recovers correctly.
corner_cases.how_to_test: Apply reset at various phases relative to clock edge during active operations. Verify clean reset and proper operation after reset release.

corner_cases.description: Sustained Full Condition: Verify FIFO remains stable when held in full state for extended period with continuous write attempts.
corner_cases.how_to_test: Fill FIFO, hold wr_en=1 with rd_en=0 for many cycles. Verify full flag stays asserted, count stays at DEPTH, no overflow or corruption.

corner_cases.description: Sustained Empty Condition: Verify FIFO remains stable when held in empty state for extended period with continuous read attempts.
corner_cases.how_to_test: Empty FIFO, hold rd_en=1 with wr_en=0 for many cycles. Verify empty flag stays asserted, count stays at 0, no underflow or rd_data corruption.

corner_cases.description: Alternating Single R/W: Verify correct operation with alternating single write and single read operations maintaining steady-state count.
corner_cases.how_to_test: Write one entry, read one entry, repeat for many cycles. Verify count oscillates between 0 and 1, flags toggle correctly, data integrity maintained.

corner_cases.description: Non-Power-of-2 Depth: Verify correct operation with DEPTH values that are not powers of 2 (e.g., 10, 17, 31).
corner_cases.how_to_test: Instantiate FIFO with non-power-of-2 DEPTH, verify pointer wraparound works correctly, count reaches exact DEPTH value, no off-by-one errors.

corner_cases.description: Simultaneous R/W in Normal State: Verify simultaneous read/write in normal state (not empty or full) performs both operations correctly.
corner_cases.how_to_test: Partially fill FIFO (count between 1 and DEPTH-1), assert both wr_en and rd_en. Verify count unchanged, write data stored, read data retrieved, pointers both advance.

corner_cases.description: Flag Transition Timing: Verify flags transition on correct clock edge relative to count changes, with no combinational glitches.
corner_cases.how_to_test: Monitor flag transitions with fine timing resolution. Verify all flags update synchronously with clock edge, no intermediate glitches during transitions.

corner_cases.description: Stress Test - Random Operations: Verify correct operation under random sequence of reads, writes, and simultaneous operations at random intervals.
corner_cases.how_to_test: Generate constrained random stimulus (respecting full/empty conditions). Run for thousands of cycles. Verify data integrity, flag correctness, count accuracy throughout.

verification_components.name: fifo_driver
verification_components.description: Drives write and read transactions to FIFO interface
responsibilities: Generate write transactions with configurable data patterns
Generate read transactions with configurable timing

verification_components.name: fifo_monitor
verification_components.description: Monitors FIFO interface and collects transactions

verification_components.name: fifo_scoreboard
responsibilities: Maintain reference model of FIFO contents
Compare read data against expected values

verification_components.name: fifo_coverage_collector
responsibilities: Cover all count values (0 to DEPTH)
Cover all operation types (write-only, read-only, simultaneous)

sequences.name: basic_write_read_seq
sequences.description: Write N entries then read N entries

sequences.name: fill_empty_seq
sequences.description: Fill FIFO completely then empty completely

assertions.name: assert_full_flag_correct
assertions.description: Full flag is asserted if and only if count equals DEPTH

assertions.name: assert_empty_flag_correct
assertions.description: Empty flag is asserted if and only if count equals 0

assertions.name: assert_almost_full_correct
assertions.description: Almost full flag is asserted if and only if count equals DEPTH-1

assertions.name: assert_almost_empty_correct
assertions.description: Almost empty flag is asserted if and only if count equals 1

assertions.name: assert_count_bounds
assertions.description: Count shall never exceed DEPTH or go below 0

assertions.name: assert_no_write_when_full
assertions.description: Write pointer shall not advance when full unless simultaneous read

assertions.name: assert_no_read_when_empty
assertions.description: Read pointer shall not advance when empty unless simultaneous write

assertions.name: assert_mutual_exclusion
assertions.description: Full and empty flags shall never be asserted simultaneously

assertions.name: assert_count_increment
assertions.description: Count increments by 1 on write-only when not full

assertions.name: assert_count_decrement
assertions.description: Count decrements by 1 on read-only when not empty

assertions.name: assert_count_stable_simul_rw
assertions.description: Count remains stable on simultaneous R/W in normal state

assertions.name: assert_reset_clears_fifo
assertions.description: Reset clears FIFO to empty state

assertions.name: assert_pointer_wraparound
assertions.description: Pointers wrap from DEPTH-1 to 0

assertions.name: assert_data_integrity
assertions.description: Data written is data read (requires reference model)
assertions.assertion: Data read matches expected value from reference FIFO model

assertions.name: assert_fifo_ordering
assertions.description: First data written is first data read
assertions.assertion: Read order matches write order (requires sequence tracking)

assertions.name: assert_count_transition
assertions.description: Count changes by at most 1 per cycle

assertions.name: assert_wr_ptr_bounds
assertions.description: Write pointer stays within valid range

assertions.name: assert_rd_ptr_bounds
assertions.description: Read pointer stays within valid range

assertions.name: assert_simultaneous_rw_empty
assertions.description: Simultaneous R/W when empty performs write only

assertions.name: assert_simultaneous_rw_full
assertions.description: Simultaneous R/W when full performs read only

coverpoints.name: cp_count
coverpoints.description: Ensure every count value is reached

testplan.description: Basic sanity test - write several entries, then read them back
Fill FIFO to capacity and verify full flag behavior
Empty FIFO completely and verify empty flag behavior
Test almost_full flag at DEPTH-1 boundary
Test almost_empty flag at count=1 boundary
Verify count signal accuracy through various operations
Test simultaneous read/write when FIFO is full
Test simultaneous read/write when FIFO is empty
Test simultaneous read/write in normal state
Test pointer wraparound from DEPTH-1 to 0
Test reset behavior and recovery
Test consecutive writes without gaps
Test consecutive reads without gaps
Test various data patterns for integrity
Test rapid transitions between full and empty
Test alternating single read/write operations
Verify all flags are consistent with count
Test with DEPTH=2 (minimum depth)
Test with non-power-of-2 DEPTH values
Test with large DATA_WIDTH (128, 256 bits)
Constrained random test with mixed operations
High-intensity stress test with random operations
